mission.title: Install and Configure Rook on Kubernetes
mission.description: Rook is a cloud-native storage orchestrator for Kubernetes that automates the deployment and management of Ceph storage. Installing Rook allows you to leverage Ceph's capabilities for file, block, and object storage in your Kubernetes clusters.

steps.title: Add the Rook Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Rook Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add rook-release https://charts.rook.io/release
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Rook using Helm
steps.description:
Install Rook in the 'rook' namespace with the following command:
```bash
helm install rook-release/rook --namespace rook --create-namespace --version v1.19.2
```

steps.title: Verify the Rook installation
steps.description:
Check that the Rook operator pod is running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n rook
```

steps.title: Set resource limits for Rook
steps.description:
Edit the Rook operator deployment to set resource limits. Use the following command to open the deployment in your editor:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment rook-operator -n rook
```
Then, add the following under 'spec.template.spec.containers.resources':
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"
```

steps.title: Check the Rook operator logs
steps.description:
To ensure that Rook is functioning correctly, check the logs of the Rook operator:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app=rook-operator -n rook
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Rook will show the Rook operator pod running in the 'rook' namespace. You should also see the resource limits applied to the operator deployment and the logs indicating that Rook is operating correctly.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add rook-release https://charts.rook.io/release
helm repo update

resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster version 1.24 or higher and have Helm and kubectl installed.
